candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A row above EBIT, but it is generic and not tied to Cost of services sold / COGS. Adding it to the selected COGS bucket would risk misclassification and double counting.
Generic operating depreciation row above EBIT, but not labelled as relating to Cost of services sold. No COGS-specific D&A row is visible.
Operating depreciation row, but not identified as belonging to Cost of services sold. It appears statement-wide rather than COGS-specific.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortisation row is labelled as belonging to Cost of services sold or an equivalent COGS bucket. Conservative approach is to assume any COGS-related D&A is already embedded in the selected COGS row, and not add any separate operating D&A row.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: This is the clearest SG&A-related D&A row, but its wording shows it is a sub-component within the already selected SG&A/G&A bucket rather than a separate same-level line to add.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: This is an R&D-specific amortization component. Because Research and Development Expenses is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, the R&D interaction rule says not to add this separately.
Separate operating D&A summary row exists, but it is broad company-level D&A and not clearly an SG&A-only amount. Using it for XSGA_DA would likely overstate and double count.

variables.notes: The explicit SG&A and R&D D&A rows shown are labelled as components inside already selected parent buckets: 'Depreciation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses' sits within SG&A/G&A, and 'Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses' sits within R&D. Since Research and Development Expenses is also already selected for XRD, adding the R&D amortization separately would distort the SG&A/XRD logic. Therefore no separate XSGA_DA row should be added.
